feed.title: Strict Programming Language Blog
feed.subtitle: The best place to stay up-to-date with the latest Strict Programming Language news and events.

entry.title: Jun 2020 As Simple As Possible
entry.summary: While exploring options yesterday and today for creating a great editor experience for Strict, I discovered some new options. We already got a VSCode integration that provides basic syntax highlighting and works to write a few lines, but is not a fun...

entry.title: Jun 2020 Back to blogging
entry.summary: My employee Abir poked me to blog more about Strict and the decisions I recently took for the IDE development. My old blog at https://BenjaminNitschke.com (2004-2016) is pretty much dead and I never found time to continue talking about Game Developme...

entry.title: May 2020 Slow and not steady
entry.summary: The corona pandemic didn't affect us much, but we did do home office work for most employees away from the Delta Engine company since mid March, for some employees things worked out great, for others it wasn't a very productive time. Also talking abo...

entry.title: Apr 2020 Sdk going open source
entry.summary: Strict was always supposed to be open source, Merlin pushed a bit to get some parts released. Upcoming work is to write the low level language framework (apps, io, logs, math, files, etc.) and to allow anyone writing any code, the strict compiler nee...

entry.title: Feb 2020 Still work in progress
entry.summary: We didn't have much time to work on the website, the language changed quite a lot into a more functional style, while still being fully declarative and test driven. The IntelliJ IDE is still being worked on and once that is finished the language will...

entry.title: Jan 2020 website is up
entry.summary: This blog is all about the development of the Strict programming language, which has the main goal allowing computers/AI to understand and write their own code with it. While Strict might look similar to C like languages or python, go, lua or lisp, c...
